patch.value: Strengthen storage, append-only history, and durability primitives (compaction, integrity, gc, export/import)
Strengthen storage, append-only history, and durability primitives (compaction, integrity, gc, export/import)
History chain integrity gates stay green; gc, compact, redact, and repair flows verified on large workspaces; no open data-loss classes.

## Charter
Living capability epic for storage, append-only history, and data durability: TOON item files, per-item history streams (hash-chained JSONL), checkpoints, gc, compaction, export/import, and format_version gating.

## Scope
- In: item-store write paths, history chain integrity (repair/redact/compact), gc scopes (locks/checkpoints/embeddings), backup/restore, format migrations.
- Out: concurrency/locking semantics (pm-dj98 owns races and atomicity), search index storage (pm-ydp6).

## Invariants this epic guards
- History is append-only and hash-chained; appendLineAtomic is the only writer (see pm-y2ml docs).
- Items must survive strict→lenient TOON decode fallback (2026-05-24 recovery precedent).
- pm health history_drift must stay at 0 across 1400+ items.

## Status
20 closed children (compact snapshot, history-compact bulk + policy, gc scopes, restore hardening). Open as a living backlog — no open children at 2026-07-04; new durability work lands here.

## Status

## Update 2026-07-06
New initiative epic parented here: **pm-9rxu — Scale-out** (sub-second, bounded-token context at 100k-1M items, thousands of concurrent agents). Owns workspace-size physics: read index, directory fan-out, pagination, claim throughput, history/git growth policy. ADR: pm-bl8x (under pm-m2u1). Boundary: pm-dj98 keeps correctness races; pm-9rxu owns throughput/scale.

## Update 2026-07-07 — immutable-proof invariant hardening
Full all-status backlog review found the append-only-proof guarantee is complete for items (restore-to-version pm-9lc, file recovery pm-g6qd, per-stream --verify, workspace drift scan) but not for workspace-level state. Two new children:
- **pm-klo8** — workspace-scoped audit history stream: schema/config/profile/init/extension-state mutations get the same append-only hash-chained JSONL treatment as items (coordinate with pm-bgcu locking; feeds pm-e200).
- **pm-9yfo** — history completeness gate: contract-enumerated test proving every mutating CLI/SDK action appends a history entry (phase 1 item-scope now; phase 2 workspace-scope after pm-klo8; shrinking-only allowlist ratchet).

value.author: codex-ecosystem-planner
value.text: Immutable-proof review 2026-07-18: the live tracker has 30,491 activity entries across 1,874 streams; every one of the 1,866 current items has at least one recorded event, and validation reports zero missing streams, hash mismatches, chain mismatches, or drifted items. Mutation-op inventory includes create/update/claim/release/close, annotations, linked artifacts, test runs, plans, compact/repair/redact, and even edit/delete operations as recorded events. Remaining guarantee extensions are already individually tracked: pm-klo8 for workspace singleton state, pm-9yfo for contract-enumerated mutation completeness, pm-4e12 for atomic multi-entity commits, pm-g5sx for branch-safe merge/reconciliation, pm-3z0k for portable attestations, and pm-u14c for principal signatures. Point-in-time non-mutating read pm-hib1 and restore/recovery foundations are shipped. No new history item needed.

value.author: codex-tracker-governance
value.text: Immutable-proof review 2026-07-21: the pre-mutation tracker exposed 31,782 activity entries across 1,909 streams; every one of the 1,901 current item ids appeared in activity, every sampled event carried before and after hashes, and workspace validation reported zero history drift. Eight history-only ids correspond to recorded delete operations rather than current items. Existing completion work remains canonical for workspace-scoped mutation coverage, contract-enumerated mutation completeness, atomic commits, branch reconciliation, portable attestations, signatures, and restore. Historical metadata debt was not rewritten with placeholders.
